title: Doc-Init Rec#5 — AGENTS.md.template refactor with framework-branching fallback
description: Refactor templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template from a TODO-heavy stub into a more useful fallback skeleton for non-AI-client users. Introduce framework-based branching so the template produces meaningful Cocos Creator / Vite / Next.js / generic variants based on {frameworkKind}. Reduce TODO count to near zero. Add a clear comment that this template is the fallback — the agents-md-init skill will produce a higher-quality version for AI-client users.

files.target: Framework-branching fallback skeleton with minimal TODO count
files.change: Add conditional sections for frameworkKind: cocos-creator variant (includes @ccclass decorator rules, .meta protection, lifecycle order, update/async ban), vite-app variant (TypeScript strict mode, composables naming, reactivity rules), next-app variant (SSR rules, hydration notes, API route conventions), generic fallback; reduce TODO count from 9+ to 0; add comment: '<!-- This is the fallback template. If you have Claude Code, run the agents-md-init skill for a semantically richer AGENTS.md. -->'
Update the template substitution call to pass framework.kind (from detector.ts result) as {frameworkKind} interpolation variable; add comment referencing fallback vs AI-skill path

implementation: Read current templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template to identify all 9+ TODO occurrences and their context
Design 4 variants: cocos-creator (Cocos-specific rules), vite-app, next-app, generic; use conditional mustache-style comments or direct variant text based on frameworkKind interpolation

Replace each TODO with framework-appropriate content (e.g., for cocos-creator: '## L0 Project Constitution — Cocos Creator {frameworkVersion}\n### Hard Rules\n- @ccclass decorator required on all Component classes...')
Add the fallback comment at top of template
Update init.ts template substitution to pass frameworkKind variable from detector result
Verify: 0 TODO occurrences remain in the cocos-creator, vite, and next variants

reference.examples: Cocos-specific rules from discussion.md Round 4 werewolf analysis: @ccclass, .meta protection, update/async ban, assets/prefabs protection

criteria: templates/agents-md/AGENTS.md.template contains 0 occurrences of '// TODO' or '<!-- TODO'
fab init on a Cocos Creator project produces AGENTS.md with @ccclass rule text (not a TODO placeholder)
fab init on a Vite project produces AGENTS.md with TypeScript strict rule text (not a TODO placeholder)
AGENTS.md output is ≤300 lines for any framework variant
Template includes fallback comment pointing to agents-md-init skill

rationale.chosen_approach: Single template file with conditional sections keeps the authoring surface simple; framework branching via interpolation variable avoids maintaining N separate template files
alternatives_considered: N separate template files per framework (more files to maintain)
Ignore fallback quality (acceptable but poor UX for non-AI users)
decision_factors: Non-AI users still benefit from a reasonable fallback
Reduces TODO wall that looks like broken output
rationale.tradeoffs: Template logic becomes slightly more complex with branching; kept simple by using text sections, not executable template engine

manual_checks: fab init on werewolf-minigame-stub — open .fabric/AGENTS.md and verify zero TODO occurrences
Verify AGENTS.md contains cocos-creator specific rules (e.g., @ccclass)
success_metrics: Zero TODO in produced AGENTS.md for all 4 framework variants

risks.description: Template branching logic too complex for simple string interpolation
risks.mitigation: Use simple approach: switch statement in init.ts selecting which template section to include, or separate template files per framework with a dispatcher in init.ts
risks.fallback: If branching is too complex, produce 4 separate template files and pick based on frameworkKind in init.ts
